text: The vocabulary of sets, relations, and functions provides a uniform language for carrying out constructions in all the branches of mathematics. Since functions and relations can be defined in terms of sets, axiomatic set theory can be used as a foundation for mathematics.
html: The vocabulary of sets, relations, and functions provides a uniform language for carrying out constructions in all the branches of mathematics. Since functions and relations can be defined in terms of sets, axiomatic set theory can be used as a foundation for mathematics.
zh: 集合、关系和函数的词汇，为数学各分支中的构造提供了一种统一语言。由于函数和关系可以用集合来定义，公理化集合论可以作为数学的基础。

text: Lean’s foundation is based instead on the primitive notion of a type , and it includes ways of defining functions between types. Every expression in Lean has a type: there are natural numbers, real numbers, functions from reals to reals, groups, vector spaces, and so on. Some expressions are types, which is to say, their type is Type . Lean and Mathlib provide ways of defining new types, and ways of defining objects of those types.
html: Lean’s foundation is based instead on the primitive notion of a type, and it includes ways of defining functions between types. Every expression in Lean has a type: there are natural numbers, real numbers, functions from reals to reals, groups, vector spaces, and so on. Some expressions are types, which is to say, their type is Type. Lean and Mathlib provide ways of defining new types, and ways of defining objects of those types.
zh: Lean 的基础则建立在类型这一原始概念之上，并包含定义类型之间函数的方法。Lean 中每个表达式都有类型：有自然数、实数、从实数到实数的函数、群、向量空间等等。有些表达式本身是类型，也就是说，它们的类型是 Type。Lean 和 Mathlib 提供了定义新类型的方法，也提供了定义这些类型中对象的方法。

text: Conceptually, you can think of a type as just a set of objects. Requiring every object to have a type has some advantages. For example, it makes it possible to overload notation like + , and it sometimes makes input less verbose because Lean can infer a lot of information from an object’s type. The type system also enables Lean to flag errors when you apply a function to the wrong number of arguments, or apply a function to arguments of the wrong type.
html: Conceptually, you can think of a type as just a set of objects. Requiring every object to have a type has some advantages. For example, it makes it possible to overload notation like +, and it sometimes makes input less verbose because Lean can infer a lot of information from an object’s type. The type system also enables Lean to flag errors when you apply a function to the wrong number of arguments, or apply a function to arguments of the wrong type.
zh: 从概念上说，可以把类型看作对象的集合。要求每个对象都有类型具有若干优点。例如，它使得像 + 这样的记号可以重载；并且有时可以减少输入的冗长程度，因为 Lean 能从对象的类型推断出大量信息。类型系统还使 Lean 能够在你把函数应用于错误数量的参数，或把函数应用于错误类型的参数时指出错误。

text: Lean’s library does define elementary set-theoretic notions. In contrast to set theory, in Lean a set is always a set of objects of some type, such as a set of natural numbers or a set of functions from real numbers to real numbers. The distinction between types and sets takes some getting used to, but this chapter will take you through the essentials.
html: Lean’s library does define elementary set-theoretic notions. In contrast to set theory, in Lean a set is always a set of objects of some type, such as a set of natural numbers or a set of functions from real numbers to real numbers. The distinction between types and sets takes some getting used to, but this chapter will take you through the essentials.
zh: Lean 的库确实定义了初等集合论概念。与集合论不同，在 Lean 中，集合总是某个类型对象的集合，例如自然数集合，或从实数到实数的函数集合。类型与集合之间的区别需要一些时间适应，但本章会带你掌握其要点。

text: If α is any type, the type Set α consists of sets of elements of α . This type supports the usual set-theoretic operations and relations. For example, s ⊆ t says that s is a subset of t , s ∩ t denotes the intersection of s and t , and s ∪ t denotes their union. The subset relation can be typed with \ss or \sub , intersection can be typed with \i or \cap , and union can be typed with \un or \cup . The library also defines the set univ , which consists of all the elements of type α , and the empty set, ∅ , which can be typed as \empty . Given x : α and s : Set α , the expression x ∈ s says that x is a member of s . Theorems that mention set membership often include mem in their name. The expression x ∉ s abbreviates ¬ x ∈ s . You can type ∈ as \in or \mem and ∉ as \notin .
html: If α is any type, the type Set α consists of sets of elements of α. This type supports the usual set-theoretic operations and relations. For example, s ⊆ t says that s is a subset of t, s ∩ t denotes the intersection of s and t, and s ∪ t denotes their union. The subset relation can be typed with \ss or \sub, intersection can be typed with \i or \cap, and union can be typed with \un or \cup. The library also defines the set univ, which consists of all the elements of type α, and the empty set, ∅, which can be typed as \empty. Given x : α and s : Set α, the expression x ∈ s says that x is a member of s. Theorems that mention set membership often include mem in their name. The expression x ∉ s abbreviates ¬ x ∈ s. You can type ∈ as \in or \mem and ∉ as \notin.
zh: 若 α 是任意类型，则类型 Set α 由 α 的元素组成的集合构成。这个类型支持通常的集合论运算和关系。例如，s ⊆ t 表示 s 是 t 的子集，s ∩ t 表示 s 与 t 的交，s ∪ t 表示它们的并。子集关系可输入为 \ss 或 \sub，交可输入为 \i 或 \cap，并可输入为 \un 或 \cup。库还定义了集合 univ，它包含类型 α 的所有元素；以及空集 ∅，可输入为 \empty。给定 x : α 和 s : Set α，表达式 x ∈ s 表示 x 是 s 的元素。提到集合成员关系的定理名中常包含 mem。表达式 x ∉ s 是 ¬ x ∈ s 的缩写。可以用 \in 或 \mem 输入 ∈，用 \notin 输入 ∉。

text: When it comes to representing sets, here is what is going on underneath the hood. In type theory, a property or predicate on a type α is just a function P : α → Prop . This makes sense: given a : α , P a is just the proposition that P holds for a . In the library, Set α is defined to be α → Prop and x ∈ s is defined to be s x . In other words, sets are really properties, treated as objects.
html: When it comes to representing sets, here is what is going on underneath the hood. In type theory, a property or predicate on a type α is just a function P : α → Prop. This makes sense: given a : α, P a is just the proposition that P holds for a. In the library, Set α is defined to be α → Prop and x ∈ s is defined to be s x. In other words, sets are really properties, treated as objects.
zh: 关于集合的表示，其底层机制如下。在类型论中，类型 α 上的性质或谓词只是函数 P : α → Prop。这很自然：给定 a : α，P a 正是断言 P 对 a 成立的命题。在库中，Set α 定义为 α → Prop，而 x ∈ s 定义为 s x。换句话说，集合其实就是性质，只是被当作对象来处理。

text: Be careful: it is somewhat confusing that the library has multiple versions of the predicate Prime . The most general one makes sense in any commutative monoid with a zero element. text: Lean introduces the notation ∀ x ∈ s, ... , “for every x in s .,” as an abbreviation for ∀ x, x ∈ s → ... . It also introduces the notation ∃ x ∈ s, ..., “there exists an x in s such that ..” These are sometimes known as bounded quantifiers , because the construction serves to restrict their significance to the set s . As a result, theorems in the library that make use of them often contain ball or bex in the name. The theorem bex_def asserts that ∃ x ∈ s, ... is equivalent to ∃ x, x ∈ s ∧ ..., but when they are used with rintro , use , and anonymous constructors, these two expressions behave roughly the same. As a result, we usually don’t need to use bex_def to transform them explicitly. Here are some examples of how they are used:

text: Indexed unions and intersections are another important set-theoretic construction. We can model a sequence \(A_0, A_1, A_2, \ldots\) of sets of elements of α as a function A : ℕ → Set α , in which case ⋃ i, A i denotes their union, and ⋂ i, A i denotes their intersection. There is nothing special about the natural numbers here, so ℕ can be replaced by any type I used to index the sets. The following illustrates their use.

There is nothing special about the natural numbers here, so ℕ can be replaced by any type I used to index the sets. The following illustrates their use.

text: Give a collection of sets, s : Set (Set α) , their union, ⋃₀ s , has type Set α and is defined as {x | ∃ t ∈ s, x ∈ t} . Similarly, their intersection, ⋂₀ s , is defined as {x | ∀ t ∈ s, x ∈ t} . These operations are called sUnion and sInter , respectively. The following examples show their relationship to bounded union and intersection.

text: If s is a set of elements of type α , the library also defines image f s , written f '' s , to be {y | ∃ x, x ∈ s ∧ f x = y} . text: To define the inverse of a function f : α → β , we will use two new ingredients. First, we need to deal with the fact that an arbitrary type in Lean may be empty. To define the inverse to f at y when there is no x satisfying f x = y , we want to assign a default value in α . Adding the annotation [Inhabited α] as a variable is tantamount to assuming that α has a preferred element, which is denoted default . Second, in the case where there is more than one x such that f x = y , the inverse function needs to choose one of them. This requires an appeal to the axiom of choice . Lean allows various ways of accessing it; one convenient method is to use the classical choose operator, illustrated below.
html: To define the inverse of a function f : α → β, we will use two new ingredients. First, we need to deal with the fact that an arbitrary type in Lean may be empty. To define the inverse to f at y when there is no x satisfying f x = y, we want to assign a default value in α. Adding the annotation [Inhabited α] as a variable is tantamount to assuming that α has a preferred element, which is denoted default. Second, in the case where there is more than one x such that f x = y, the inverse function needs to choose one of them. This requires an appeal to the axiom of choice. Lean allows various ways of accessing it; one convenient method is to use the classical choose operator, illustrated below.
zh: 要定义函数 f : α → β 的逆，我们将使用两个新成分。首先，需要处理 Lean 中任意类型可能为空这一事实。当不存在满足 f x = y 的 x 时，要定义 f 在 y 处的逆值，我们希望给 α 中指定一个默认值。把标注 [Inhabited α] 作为变量加入，就等同于假定 α 有一个首选元素，记作 default。其次，在有多个 x 满足 f x = y 的情况下，逆函数需要从中选择一个。这需要诉诸选择公理。Lean 允许以多种方式使用它；一种方便方法是使用经典的 choose 运算符，如下所示。

text: The lines noncomputable section and open Classical are needed because we are using classical logic in an essential way. On input y , the function inverse f returns some value of x satisfying f x = y if there is one, and a default element of α otherwise. This is an instance of a dependent if construction, since in the positive case, the value returned, Classical.choose h , depends on the assumption h . The identity dif_pos h rewrites if h : e then a else b to a given h : e , and, similarly, dif_neg h rewrites it to b given h : ¬ e . There are also versions if_pos and if_neg that works for non-dependent if constructions and will be used in the next section. The theorem inverse_spec says that inverse f meets the first part of this specification.

On input y, the function inverse f returns some value of x satisfying f x = y if there is one, and a default element of α otherwise. This is an instance of a dependent if construction, since in the positive case, the value returned, Classical.choose h, depends on the assumption h. The identity dif_pos h rewrites if h : e then a else b to a given h : e, and, similarly, dif_neg h rewrites it to b given h : ¬ e. There are also versions if_pos and if_neg that works for non-dependent if constructions and will be used in the next section. The theorem inverse_spec says that inverse f meets the first part of this specification.

text: Don’t worry if you do not fully understand how these work. text: We close this section with a type-theoretic statement of Cantor’s famous theorem that there is no surjective function from a set to its power set. See if you can understand the proof, and then fill in the two lines that are missing.

text: We close this chapter with an elementary but nontrivial theorem of set theory. Let \(\alpha\) and \(\beta\) be sets. (In our formalization, they will actually be types.) Suppose \(f : \alpha → \beta\) and \(g : \beta → \alpha\) are both injective. Intuitively, this means that \(\alpha\) is no bigger than \(\beta\) and vice-versa. If \(\alpha\) and \(\beta\) are finite, this implies that they have the same cardinality, which is equivalent to saying that there is a bijection between them. In the nineteenth century, Cantor stated that same result holds even in the case where \(\alpha\) and \(\beta\) are infinite. This was eventually established by Dedekind, Schröder, and Bernstein independently.
html: We close this chapter with an elementary but nontrivial theorem of set theory. Let \(\alpha\) and \(\beta\) be sets. (In our formalization, they will actually be types.) Suppose \(f : \alpha → \beta\) and \(g : \beta → \alpha\) are both injective. Intuitively, this means that \(\alpha\) is no bigger than \(\beta\) and vice-versa. If \(\alpha\) and \(\beta\) are finite, this implies that they have the same cardinality, which is equivalent to saying that there is a bijection between them. In the nineteenth century, Cantor stated that same result holds even in the case where \(\alpha\) and \(\beta\) are infinite. This was eventually established by Dedekind, Schröder, and Bernstein independently.
zh: 本章最后以集合论中的一个初等但非平凡的定理收尾。设 \(\alpha\) 和 \(\beta\) 为集合。（在我们的形式化中，它们实际上是类型。）假设 \(f : \alpha \to \beta\) 和 \(g : \beta \to \alpha\) 都是单射。直观地说，这意味着 \(\alpha\) 不大于 \(\beta\)，反之亦然。如果 \(\alpha\) 和 \(\beta\) 有限，这就蕴含它们有相同基数，等价地说，二者之间存在一个双射。十九世纪时，Cantor 陈述了即使在 \(\alpha\) 和 \(\beta\) 无限时同样成立的结果。后来 Dedekind、Schröder 和 Bernstein 分别独立证明了它。

text: Our formalization will introduce some new methods that we will explain in greater detail in chapters to come. Don’t worry if they go by too quickly here. Our goal is to show you that you already have the skills to contribute to the formal proof of a real mathematical result.
html: Our formalization will introduce some new methods that we will explain in greater detail in chapters to come. Don’t worry if they go by too quickly here. Our goal is to show you that you already have the skills to contribute to the formal proof of a real mathematical result.
zh: 我们的形式化将引入一些新方法，后续章节会更详细解释。如果这里讲得太快，也不必担心。我们的目标是让你看到，你已经具备为真实数学结果的形式化证明作出贡献的技能。

text: To understand the idea behind the proof, consider the image of the map \(g\) in \(\alpha\) . On that image, the inverse of \(g\) is defined and is a bijection with \(\beta\) .
html: To understand the idea behind the proof, consider the image of the map \(g\) in \(\alpha\). On that image, the inverse of \(g\) is defined and is a bijection with \(\beta\).
zh: 为了理解证明背后的思想，考虑映射 \(g\) 在 \(\alpha\) 中的像。在这个像上，\(g\) 的逆是有定义的，并且给出它与 \(\beta\) 的一个双射。

text: The problem is that the bijection does not include the shaded region in the diagram, which is nonempty if \(g\) is not surjective. Alternatively, we can use \(f\) to map all of \(\alpha\) to \(\beta\) , but in that case the problem is that if \(f\) is not surjective, it will miss some elements of \(\beta\) .
html: The problem is that the bijection does not include the shaded region in the diagram, which is nonempty if \(g\) is not surjective. Alternatively, we can use \(f\) to map all of \(\alpha\) to \(\beta\), but in that case the problem is that if \(f\) is not surjective, it will miss some elements of \(\beta\).
zh: 问题在于，如果 \(g\) 不是满射，这个双射没有覆盖图中阴影区域；该区域非空。另一种做法是用 \(f\) 把整个 \(\alpha\) 映到 \(\beta\)，但那样的问题是，如果 \(f\) 不是满射，就会遗漏 \(\beta\) 中的一些元素。

text: But now consider the composition \(g \circ f\) from \(\alpha\) to itself. Because the composition is injective, it forms a bijection between \(\alpha\) and its image, yielding a scaled-down copy of \(\alpha\) inside itself.
html: But now consider the composition \(g \circ f\) from \(\alpha\) to itself. Because the composition is injective, it forms a bijection between \(\alpha\) and its image, yielding a scaled-down copy of \(\alpha\) inside itself.
zh: 现在考虑从 \(\alpha\) 到自身的复合 \(g \circ f\)。因为该复合是单射，它在 \(\alpha\) 与其像之间形成一个双射，从而在 \(\alpha\) 内部得到一个缩小的 \(\alpha\) 副本。

text: This composition maps the inner shaded ring to yet another such set, which we can think of as an even smaller concentric shaded ring, and so on. This yields a concentric sequence of shaded rings, each of which is in bijective correspondence with the next. If we map each ring to the next and leave the unshaded parts of \(\alpha\) alone, we have a bijection of \(\alpha\) with the image of \(g\) . Composing with \(g^{-1}\) , this yields the desired bijection between \(\alpha\) and \(\beta\) .
html: This composition maps the inner shaded ring to yet another such set, which we can think of as an even smaller concentric shaded ring, and so on. This yields a concentric sequence of shaded rings, each of which is in bijective correspondence with the next. If we map each ring to the next and leave the unshaded parts of \(\alpha\) alone, we have a bijection of \(\alpha\) with the image of \(g\). Composing with \(g^{-1}\), this yields the desired bijection between \(\alpha\) and \(\beta\).
zh: 这个复合把内侧阴影环映到另一个同类集合，我们可以把它看作更小的同心阴影环，如此继续。于是得到一个同心的阴影环序列，每个环都与下一个环双射对应。如果把每个环映到下一个环，并保持 \(\alpha\) 的非阴影部分不变，就得到 \(\alpha\) 与 \(g\) 的像之间的一个双射。再与 \(g^{-1}\) 复合，就得到所需的 \(\alpha\) 与 \(\beta\) 之间的双射。

text: We can describe this bijection more simply. Let \(A\) be the union of the sequence of shaded regions, and define \(h : \alpha \to \beta\) as follows:
html: We can describe this bijection more simply. Let \(A\) be the union of the sequence of shaded regions, and define \(h : \alpha \to \beta\) as follows:
zh: 可以更简单地描述这个双射。设 \(A\) 为阴影区域序列的并，并如下定义 \(h : \alpha \to \beta\)：

text: In other words, we use \(f\) on the shaded parts, and we use the inverse of \(g\) everywhere else. The resulting map \(h\) is injective because each component is injective and the images of the two components are disjoint. To see that it is surjective, suppose we are given a \(y\) in \(\beta\) , and consider \(g(y)\) . If \(g(y)\) is in one of the shaded regions, it cannot be in the first ring, so we have \(g(y) = g(f(x))\) for some \(x\) is in the previous ring. By the injectivity of \(g\) , we have \(h(x) = f(x) = y\) . If \(g(y)\) is not in the shaded region, then by the definition of \(h\) , we have \(h(g(y))= y\) . Either way, \(y\) is in the image of \(h\) .
html: In other words, we use \(f\) on the shaded parts, and we use the inverse of \(g\) everywhere else. The resulting map \(h\) is injective because each component is injective and the images of the two components are disjoint. To see that it is surjective, suppose we are given a \(y\) in \(\beta\), and consider \(g(y)\). If \(g(y)\) is in one of the shaded regions, it cannot be in the first ring, so we have \(g(y) = g(f(x))\) for some \(x\) is in the previous ring. By the injectivity of \(g\), we have \(h(x) = f(x) = y\). If \(g(y)\) is not in the shaded region, then by the definition of \(h\), we have \(h(g(y))= y\). Either way, \(y\) is in the image of \(h\).
zh: 换言之，在阴影部分使用 \(f\)，在其他地方使用 \(g\) 的逆。所得映射 \(h\) 是单射，因为每个组成部分都是单射，且两个组成部分的像互不相交。要看它是满射，设给定 \(\beta\) 中的 \(y\)，并考虑 \(g(y)\)。若 \(g(y)\) 位于某个阴影区域，则它不能在第一个环中，所以有 \(g(y) = g(f(x))\)，其中 \(x\) 位于前一个环中。由 \(g\) 的单射性，得到 \(h(x) = f(x) = y\)。若 \(g(y)\) 不在阴影区域，则由 \(h\) 的定义有 \(h(g(y)) = y\)。无论哪种情况，\(y\) 都在 \(h\) 的像中。

text: This argument should sound plausible, but the details are delicate. Formalizing the proof will not only improve our confidence in the result, but also help us understand it better. Because the proof uses classical logic, we tell Lean that our definitions will generally not be computable.
html: This argument should sound plausible, but the details are delicate. Formalizing the proof will not only improve our confidence in the result, but also help us understand it better. Because the proof uses classical logic, we tell Lean that our definitions will generally not be computable.
zh: 这个论证听起来应当是可信的，但细节相当微妙。形式化证明不仅会增强我们对结果的信心，也会帮助我们更好地理解它。由于证明使用经典逻辑，我们告诉 Lean：我们的定义通常不是可计算的。

text: The annotation [Nonempty β] specifies that β is nonempty. We use it because the Mathlib primitive that we will use to construct \(g^{-1}\) requires it. The case of the theorem where \(\beta\) is empty is trivial, and even though it would not be hard to generalize the formalization to cover that case as well, we will not bother. Specifically, we need the hypothesis [Nonempty β] for the operation invFun that is defined in Mathlib. Given x : α , invFun g x chooses a preimage of x in β if there is one, and returns an arbitrary element of β otherwise. The function invFun g is always a left inverse if g is injective and a right inverse if g is surjective.
html: The annotation [Nonempty β] specifies that β is nonempty. We use it because the Mathlib primitive that we will use to construct \(g^{-1}\) requires it. The case of the theorem where \(\beta\) is empty is trivial, and even though it would not be hard to generalize the formalization to cover that case as well, we will not bother. Specifically, we need the hypothesis [Nonempty β] for the operation invFun that is defined in Mathlib. Given x : α, invFun g x chooses a preimage of x in β if there is one, and returns an arbitrary element of β otherwise. The function invFun g is always a left inverse if g is injective and a right inverse if g is surjective.
zh: 标注 [Nonempty β] 指明 β 非空。我们使用它，是因为将要用来构造 \(g^{-1}\) 的 Mathlib 原语需要这一点。当 \(\beta\) 为空时，定理的情形是平凡的；虽然把形式化推广到涵盖该情形并不困难，但我们不费这个功夫。具体地说，我们需要假设 [Nonempty β] 来使用 Mathlib 中定义的运算 invFun。给定 x : α，invFun g x 在存在 x 在 β 中的原像时选择一个这样的原像，否则返回 β 的任意元素。若 g 是单射，则函数 invFun g 总是一个左逆；若 g 是满射，则它总是一个右逆。

text: We will need the fact that our definition of \(g^{-1}\) is a right inverse on the complement of \(A\) , which is to say, on the non-shaded regions of \(\alpha\) . This is so because the outermost ring, \(S_0\) , is equal to \(\alpha \setminus g(\beta)\) , so the complement of \(A\) is contained in \(g(\beta)\) . As a result, for every \(x\) in the complement of \(A\) , there is a \(y\) such that \(g(y) = x\) . (By the injectivity of \(g\) , this \(y\) is unique, but next theorem says only that invFun g x returns some y such that g y = x .)
html: We will need the fact that our definition of \(g^{-1}\) is a right inverse on the complement of \(A\), which is to say, on the non-shaded regions of \(\alpha\). This is so because the outermost ring, \(S_0\), is equal to \(\alpha \setminus g(\beta)\), so the complement of \(A\) is contained in \(g(\beta)\). As a result, for every \(x\) in the complement of \(A\), there is a \(y\) such that \(g(y) = x\). (By the injectivity of \(g\), this \(y\) is unique, but next theorem says only that invFun g x returns some y such that g y = x.)
zh: 我们将需要这样一个事实：我们对 \(g^{-1}\) 的定义，在 \(A\) 的补集上是右逆，也就是说，在 \(\alpha\) 的非阴影区域上是右逆。这是因为最外侧环 \(S_0\) 等于 \(\alpha \setminus g(\beta)\)，所以 \(A\) 的补集包含于 \(g(\beta)\)。因此，对补集中的每个 \(x\)，都存在 \(y\) 使得 \(g(y) = x\)。（由 \(g\) 的单射性，这个 \(y\) 是唯一的；但下一个定理只说明 invFun g x 返回某个满足 g y = x 的 y。）

text: We now turn to the proof that \(h\) is injective. Informally, the proof goes as follows. First, suppose \(h(x_1) = h(x_2)\) . If \(x_1\) is in \(A\) , then \(h(x_1) = f(x_1)\) , and we can show that \(x_2\) is in \(A\) as follows. If it isn’t, then we have \(h(x_2) = g^{-1}(x_2)\) . From \(f(x_1) = h(x_1) = h(x_2)\) we have \(g(f(x_1)) = x_2\) . From the definition of \(A\) , since \(x_1\) is in \(A\) , \(x_2\) is in \(A\) as well, a contradiction. Hence, if \(x_1\) is in \(A\) , so is \(x_2\) , in which case we have \(f(x_1) = h(x_1) = h(x_2) = f(x_2)\) . The injectivity of \(f\) then implies \(x_1 = x_2\) . The symmetric argument shows that if \(x_2\) is in \(A\) , then so is \(x_1\) , which again implies \(x_1 = x_2\) .
html: We now turn to the proof that \(h\) is injective. Informally, the proof goes as follows. First, suppose \(h(x_1) = h(x_2)\). If \(x_1\) is in \(A\), then \(h(x_1) = f(x_1)\), and we can show that \(x_2\) is in \(A\) as follows. If it isn’t, then we have \(h(x_2) = g^{-1}(x_2)\). From \(f(x_1) = h(x_1) = h(x_2)\) we have \(g(f(x_1)) = x_2\). From the definition of \(A\), since \(x_1\) is in \(A\), \(x_2\) is in \(A\) as well, a contradiction. Hence, if \(x_1\) is in \(A\), so is \(x_2\), in which case we have \(f(x_1) = h(x_1) = h(x_2) = f(x_2)\). The injectivity of \(f\) then implies \(x_1 = x_2\). The symmetric argument shows that if \(x_2\) is in \(A\), then so is \(x_1\), which again implies \(x_1 = x_2\).
zh: 现在转向证明 \(h\) 是单射。非形式地说，证明如下。首先，假设 \(h(x_1) = h(x_2)\)。若 \(x_1\) 在 \(A\) 中，则 \(h(x_1) = f(x_1)\)，并且可以如下证明 \(x_2\) 也在 \(A\) 中。若它不在，则 \(h(x_2) = g^{-1}(x_2)\)。由 \(f(x_1) = h(x_1) = h(x_2)\) 得到 \(g(f(x_1)) = x_2\)。根据 \(A\) 的定义，由于 \(x_1\) 在 \(A\) 中，\(x_2\) 也在 \(A\) 中，矛盾。因此，若 \(x_1\) 在 \(A\) 中，则 \(x_2\) 也在；此时有 \(f(x_1) = h(x_1) = h(x_2) = f(x_2)\)。由 \(f\) 的单射性推出 \(x_1 = x_2\)。对称论证表明，若 \(x_2\) 在 \(A\) 中，则 \(x_1\) 也在，从而同样推出 \(x_1 = x_2\)。

text: The only remaining possibility is that neither \(x_1\) nor \(x_2\) is in \(A\) . In that case, we have \(g^{-1}(x_1) = h(x_1) = h(x_2) = g^{-1}(x_2)\) . Applying \(g\) to both sides yields \(x_1 = x_2\) .
html: The only remaining possibility is that neither \(x_1\) nor \(x_2\) is in \(A\). In that case, we have \(g^{-1}(x_1) = h(x_1) = h(x_2) = g^{-1}(x_2)\). Applying \(g\) to both sides yields \(x_1 = x_2\).
zh: 唯一剩下的可能性是 \(x_1\) 与 \(x_2\) 都不在 \(A\) 中。在这种情况下，有 \(g^{-1}(x_1) = h(x_1) = h(x_2) = g^{-1}(x_2)\)。对等式两边应用 \(g\)，得到 \(x_1 = x_2\)。

text: The proof introduces some new tactics. To start with, notice the set tactic, which introduces abbreviations A and h for sbSet f g and sb_fun f g respectively. We name the corresponding defining equations A_def and h_def . The abbreviations are definitional, which is to say, Lean will sometimes unfold them automatically when needed. But not always; for example, when using rw , we generally need to use A_def and h_def explicitly. So the definitions bring a tradeoff: they can make expressions shorter and more readable, but they sometimes require us to do more work.

text: The argument for surjectivity is even easier. Given \(y\) in \(\beta\) , we consider two cases, depending on whether \(g(y)\) is in \(A\) . If it is, it can’t be in \(S_0\) , the outermost ring, because by definition that is disjoint from the image of \(g\) . Thus it is an element of \(S_{n+1}\) for some \(n\) . This means that it is of the form \(g(f(x))\) for some \(x\) in \(S_n\) . By the injectivity of \(g\) , we have \(f(x) = y\) . In the case where \(g(y)\) is in the complement of \(A\) , we immediately have \(h(g(y))= y\) , and we are done.
html: The argument for surjectivity is even easier. Given \(y\) in \(\beta\), we consider two cases, depending on whether \(g(y)\) is in \(A\). If it is, it can’t be in \(S_0\), the outermost ring, because by definition that is disjoint from the image of \(g\). Thus it is an element of \(S_{n+1}\) for some \(n\). This means that it is of the form \(g(f(x))\) for some \(x\) in \(S_n\). By the injectivity of \(g\), we have \(f(x) = y\). In the case where \(g(y)\) is in the complement of \(A\), we immediately have \(h(g(y))= y\), and we are done.
zh: 满射性的证明更容易。给定 \(\beta\) 中的 \(y\)，我们根据 \(g(y)\) 是否在 \(A\) 中分两种情况。如果在，它不可能位于最外侧环 \(S_0\)，因为按定义该环与 \(g\) 的像不相交。因此，它对某个 \(n\) 是 \(S_{n+1}\) 的元素。这意味着它形如 \(g(f(x))\)，其中 \(x\) 在 \(S_n\) 中。由 \(g\) 的单射性，得到 \(f(x) = y\)。若 \(g(y)\) 在 \(A\) 的补集中，则立即有 \(h(g(y)) = y\)，证明完成。
